# Statuses

[![NPM Version][npm-version-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][npm-downloads-image]][npm-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][build-image]][build-url]
[![Test Coverage][coverage-image]][coverage-url]

HTTP status utility for Node.js

This module provides a utility to work with HTTP status codes. It includes helper methods to determine if a status code is of a certain class (e.g., "client error"), as well as a utility to convert between status codes and their corresponding status messages.

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install statuses
```

## API

```js
var status = require('statuses')
```

### status(code)

Returns the numeric status code from a status code or message.

```js
status(403) // => 403
status('403') // => 403
status('Forbidden') // => 403
```

Throws if the status code does not exist.

### status.STATUS_CODES

A map of status codes to status messages, like Node.js's [http.STATUS_CODES](https://nodejs.org/dist/latest/docs/api/http.html#http_http_status_codes).

```js
status.STATUS_CODES[404] // => 'Not Found'
```

### status.codes

An array of all the status codes as integers.

### status.redirect

An object of status codes that are redirects (3xx).

```js
status.redirect[307] // => true
```

### status.empty

An object of status codes that represent an empty body (204, 205, 304).

```js
status.empty[204] // => true
```

### status.retry

An object of status codes that should be retried (502, 503, 504).

```js
status.retry[503] // => true
```

### status.isInformational(code)

Returns true if a status code is an informational status (1xx).

```js
status.isInformational(103) // => true
status.isInformational(200) // => false
```

### status.isSuccess(code)

Returns true if a status code is a success status (2xx).

```js
status.isSuccess(200) // => true
status.isSuccess(404) // => false
```

### status.isRedirect(code)

Returns true if a status code is a redirect status (3xx).

```js
status.isRedirect(301) // => true
status.isRedirect(200) // => false
```

### status.isClientError(code)

Returns true if a status code is a client error status (4xx).

```js
status.isClientError(400) // => true
status.isClientError(500) // => false
```

### status.isServerError(code)

Returns true if a status code is a server error status (5xx).

```js
status.isServerError(500) // => true
status.isServerError(400) // => false
```

### status.isError(code)

Returns true if a status code is a client or server error status (4xx or 5xx).

```js
status.isError(429) // => true
status.isError(503) // => true
status.isError(200) // => false
```

### status.isEmpty(code)

Returns true if a status code expects an empty body (204, 205, 304).

```js
status.isEmpty(204) // => true
status.isEmpty(200) // => false
```

### status.isRetry(code)

Returns true if a status code is a retry status (502, 503, 504).

```js
status.isRetry(503) // => true
status.isRetry(500) // => false
```
